task: I’m drafting a reply to a vendor about a test failure, and the explanation they sent doesn’t seem to line up with the evidence. Can you help me write a short, professional note that asks for the missing details and keeps the discussion focused on what can actually be verified?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, professional version you can use:

> Thanks for the update. The explanation doesn’t seem to match the evidence we have. Could you share the exact test condition that failed to reproduce, what you already checked on your side, and the specific next action you recommend? Please keep it focused on verifiable facts so we can align on the issue.

If you want, I can also make it firmer or more diplomatic.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but I’d want it a bit tighter and less soft. It still sounds like we’re asking nicely instead of forcing them to pin it down.

Give me a version that says: their explanation doesn’t reconcile with the data, we need the exact failed condition, what they ruled out, and what evidence supports their conclusion. No filler, no speculation.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter version:

> Their explanation doesn’t reconcile with the data. Please provide the exact condition that failed to reproduce, what you ruled out on your side, and the evidence supporting your conclusion. Keep it to verified facts only—no filler or speculation.

If you want, I can make it even more direct or turn it into a full email.

user_simulator.text:
That works. Tight enough.

I’d use this:

> Their explanation does not reconcile with the data. Please provide the exact failed condition, what you ruled out on your side, and the evidence supporting your conclusion. Keep it to verified facts only.

Cleaner. No extra padding.
